text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是Neil。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是Georgie。

text: Neil, have you ever ridden a horse?
zh: Neil，你骑过马吗？

text: Yes, I have.
zh: 骑过。

text: I can't say that I enjoyed it much.
zh: 说实话，我不能说我很享受那次经历。

text: I was a bit scared, to be honest.
zh: 老实说，我当时有点害怕。

text: How about you?
zh: 你呢？

text: I used to ride horses a lot, actually.
zh: 其实我以前经常骑马。

text: My favourite thing was to ride a horse through the countryside.
zh: 我最喜欢的事情就是骑马穿越乡野。

text: I love horses, they're so majestic and lovely.
zh: 我很喜欢马，它们既威严又可爱。

text: The relationship between humans and horses has been close throughout history, but recently this has gone beyond riding horses for fun or using them for work.
zh: 人类与马的关系自古以来就很密切，但最近这种关系已经超越了骑马娱乐或用马劳作的范畴。

text: Equine Assisted Services, sometimes called horse therapy, is a health trend where caring and interacting with horses is used to heal physical and mental illness.
zh: “马匹辅助服务”，有时也叫“马术治疗”，是一种健康新趋势，通过照料和与马互动来治疗身心疾病。

text: Here's Daciani Navanayaga, presenter of BBC World Service programme The Conversation, to explain.
zh: 下面请BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》主持人Daciani Navanayaga为我们讲解。

text: explain.
zh: 讲解一下。

text: Equine Assisted Services is now an umbrella term which encompasses a whole range of treatments involving activities and interactions with horses to promote better human wellbeing.
zh: “马匹辅助服务”如今是一个统称，涵盖了一系列通过与马互动来促进人类健康的活动和疗法。

text: Equine Assisted Services is an umbrella term – a term for a large number of different things which all belong to a single common category.
zh: “马匹辅助服务”是一个统称——用来指代一大类共同归属于同一大类别的不同事物。

text: So, horse therapy includes many activities – from feeling the horse's movement, which can help children with muscle disorders like cerebral palsy to simply grooming the horse, which can help calm anxiety.
zh: 因此，马术治疗包括许多活动——从感受马的movement（动作），有助于患有脑瘫等肌肉疾病的儿童，到简单地为马梳毛，这有助于缓解焦虑。

text: Grooming a horse means cleaning and brushing the horse's coat of hair.
zh: 给马梳毛（grooming）指的是清洁和刷理马的毛发。

text: Various animals can be used in therapy, but horses are particularly suitable as they're very sensitive to human emotions.
zh: 多种动物都可以用于治疗，但马尤其合适，因为它们对人类情绪非常敏感。

text: Being in contact with them affects the limbic area of the brain dealing with stress.
zh: 与马接触会影响大脑中处理压力的边缘系统区域。

text: In this episode we'll hear more about the horses helping people work through mental, emotional and physical challenges.
zh: 在本期节目中，我们将进一步了解马如何帮助人们克服心理、情感和身体上的困难。

text: And as usual, we'll learn some useful new vocabulary.
zh: 和往常一样，我们也会学习一些实用的新词汇。

text: And remember, you'll find all the words and phrases from this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住，本期节目中的所有单词和短语都可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: But now I have a question for you, Neil.
zh: 不过现在，我有一个问题要考考你，Neil。

text: We've heard some of the special qualities horses possess, but which of the following about horses is also true?
zh: 我们已经听到了一些关于马的特殊之处，那么下面关于马的说法中，哪一个是正确的呢？

text: Is it…
a Horses always sleep standing up.
zh: A. 马总是站着睡觉。

text: b Horses can make over 100 sounds.
zh: B. 马能发出100多种声音。

text: or C. Horses have almost 360 degree vision?
zh: 还是C. 马几乎拥有360度的视野？

text: Well, I'm sure I've seen a horse lying down, so I don't know about its vision though, so I'm going to say B. A horse can make over 100 sounds.
zh: 我确实见过躺着的马，所以关于视野我就不确定了，我选B，马能发出100多种声音。

text: OK, well we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的，我们稍后节目中会揭晓答案。

text: Horse therapist Claudia Nicholson first felt the healing power of horses as a child.
zh: 马术治疗师Claudia Nicholson小时候就第一次感受到马的治愈力量。

text: She now runs her own centre for equine assisted learning in the south of England, where she spoke with BBC World Service programme The Conversation.
zh: 她如今在英格兰南部经营着自己的马匹辅助学习中心，并接受了BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》的采访。

text: I would spend hours on end sitting in a stable with my pony.
zh: 我常常一连几个小时坐在马厩里陪着我的小马。

text: I was going through puberty, my parents had separated,
zh: 那时我正值青春期，父母也分开了，

text: I had a lot of unanswered questions and I would feel met and safe and held with the connection of the pony.
zh: 我心中有很多得不到答案的疑问，而与小马的联系让我感到被理解、被安全地包容着。

text: As a child, Claudia spent hours on end with her pony.
zh: 小时候，Claudia常常一连几个小时和她的小马待在一起。

text: Phrases like days or hours on end mean days or hours doing something continuously without stopping.
zh: 像days on end或hours on end这样的短语，意思是连续不断地做某事，一天天或一小时又一小时地持续下去。

text: Spending time with her pony helped Claudia through puberty, the stage of life when a child grows into an adult because of changes in their body which allow them to have children.
zh: 和小马相处帮助Claudia度过了青春期——也就是儿童因身体变化而逐渐成长为能够生育后代的成年人的阶段。

text: It was her teenage experiences that led Claudia to start her own horse therapy centre.
zh: 正是青少年时期的经历，促使Claudia创办了自己的马术治疗中心。

text: She works a lot with teenage girls and children in care.
zh: 她的服务对象主要是青少年女孩和寄养儿童。

text: A child in care is a child who's being looked after by the local authorities because they cannot be cared for by their parents or family.
zh: “寄养儿童”指的是由于无法得到父母或家人照顾，而由地方当局负责照料的儿童。

text: Here, Claudia talks more about her work with children and horses to BBC World Service's The Conversation.
zh: 在这里，Claudia向BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》进一步讲述了她与孩子和马匹相处的工作。

text: It would take maybe a few sessions
zh: 大概需要几次疗程，

text: for a relationship of trust to be formed and it's quite extraordinary to see a child that's experienced a lot of trauma,
zh: 信任关系才能建立起来，而看到一个经历过很多创伤的孩子发生变化，真的令人惊叹，

text: especially these children in care.
zh: 尤其是这些寄养儿童。

text: They can be brushing the pony's body.
zh: 他们可能正在为小马刷毛，

text: They might want to have a cry which they've been holding on to.
zh: 这时他们可能会突然忍不住哭出来，那是他们一直压抑着的情绪。

text: The horse regulates them.
zh: 马能让他们平静下来。

text: Many of the children Claudia works with suffer trauma.
zh: Claudia所接触的许多孩子都曾经历过创伤。

text: It takes time for them to build a bond of trust with their horse.
zh: 他们需要时间才能和马建立起信任的纽带。

text: The phrase bond of trust is a strong feeling of reliance and confidence between two people.
zh: “bond of trust”（信任的纽带）指的是两个人之间强烈的依赖和信任感。

text: Horses really are special animals.
zh: 马真的是一种特别的动物。

text: They sense human feelings like fear or sadness through breath, heart rate and movement, and respond with gentleness and calm.
zh: 它们能通过呼吸、心率和动作感知人类的情绪，比如恐惧或悲伤，并以温柔和平静作出回应。

text: This makes them perfect animal therapists.
zh: 这使它们成为完美的“动物治疗师”。

text: And that's not all, right Georgie?
zh: 这还不是全部，对吧，Georgie？

text: Let's find out the answer to your question.
zh: 我们来揭晓你问题的答案吧。

text: Yes, Neil.
zh: 好的，Neil。

text: Well, I asked you which sentence about horses was true, and you said that horses can make over 100 sounds.
zh: 我之前问你，关于马的哪个说法是正确的，你回答说马能发出100多种声音。

text: Sadly, that was the wrong answer.
zh: 很遗憾，这个答案是错的。

text: The correct answer was C – horses have near 360 degree vision, which is surprising.
zh: 正确答案是C——马几乎拥有360度的视野，这真是令人惊讶。

text: Wow, that's amazing.
zh: 哇，太神奇了。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with umbrella term, a term used to describe many different things which all belong to a single common category.
zh: 好了，我们来复习一下今天学到的词汇，首先是umbrella term（统称），指用来描述一大类共同归属于同一类别的不同事物的词语。

text: Grooming an animal, like a horse or a dog, means brushing and cleaning its coat of hair.
zh: grooming an animal（给动物梳毛），比如马或狗，指的是刷理和清洁它们的毛发。

text: If you do something for hours on end, you do it continuously without stopping.
zh: 如果你hours on end（连续不断地）做某事，意味着你不间断地持续做这件事。

text: Puberty is the stage of life when a child grows into an adult because of changes in their body which allow them to have children.
zh: puberty（青春期）是指儿童因身体变化而逐渐发育为能够生育后代的成年人的阶段。

text: A child who is in care is being looked after by the local authorities because they cannot be cared for by their parents.
zh: a child in care（受照护儿童）指的是因无法得到父母照顾，而由地方当局负责照料的儿童。

text: And finally, a bond of trust is a strong feeling of reliance and confidence between two people.
zh: 最后，bond of trust（信任的纽带）指的是两个人之间强烈的依赖和信任感。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you'll find a quiz and worksheet for this episode over on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了，不过别忘了，你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的测验和练习题。

text: See you there soon, but for now it's goodbye.
zh: 我们很快在那里见，不过现在，先说再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
